claimText: Clinidium lalitae is a newly identified species of beetle discovered in the Talle Valley Wildlife Sanctuary in Arunachal Pradesh.
evidenceExcerpt: Scientists from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) identified a new species of beetle named Clinidium lalitae, in Talle Valley Wildlife Sanctuary, Arunachal Pradesh.
examUse: Species-Location matching questions or statement-based questions on recent discoveries.

claimText: Clinidium lalitae belongs to the Rhysodinae subfamily, which is a rare group of ground beetles that inhabit forest floors.
evidenceExcerpt: It belongs to the subfamily Rhysodinae, a rare group of ground beetles inhibiting forest floors
examUse: Statement-based questions regarding the habitat and classification of the species.

claimText: The Clinidium lalitae beetle contributes significantly to nutrient cycling and soil health in its ecosystem.
evidenceExcerpt: plays a significant ecological role in nutrient cycling and soil health.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the ecological significance of newly discovered ground beetles.
distractorHooks: Invasive pest that degrades soil quality
